text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little town called Willowgrove, there lived a wise old carpenter named Elias. His workshop was a haven of crafts: wooden chests, intricate dollhouses, and even a peculiar wooden owl named Owley that never slept.

Elias was known far and wide for a peculiar hobby—he enjoyed telling stories. Especially to children about a place called Nevereverland where anything was possible.

One autumn evening, as the golden leaves danced on the wind, a little girl named Lila skipped into Elias’s workshop. Her cheeks were pink with excitement.

“Elias,” she called up the tall ladder, “tell me a story about Nevereverland!”

With a smile, Elias picked up a smooth, round wooden ball and began.

“It begins with you, Lila. You wander through a meadow filled with colors you’ve never seen and hear music from a hidden brook.”

He spun the ball, and it began to tell its own tale.

“There’s a singing
